Documentation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":""},{"id":2971442611,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyOTcxNDQyNjEx","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/labels/sprintable","name":"sprintable","color":"5319e7","default":false,"description":"Issue fits the time-frame and setting of a sprint"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":27,"created_at":"2022-04-17T03:48:14Z","updated_at":"2024-09-23T06:30:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":""Examples, more examples, and more detailed examples" is the recurrent theme in the feedback about the NumPy documentation we received via the 2020 and 2021 NumPy user surveys. 

If you come across a docstring where a function is missing an example or more/better examples are needed, please add a comment below.

**Re: the “sprintable” label** This could be a good activity for a sprint with new contributors as most of them are NumPy users. However, one doesn’t have to be a sprint participant to contribute to this initiative.

Please do not work on this unless you're participating to a sprint"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2023-06-22T07:54:56Z","updated_at":"2023-12-09T12:04:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Issue with current documentation:

Documentation: https://scipy.github.io/devdocs/reference/optimize.qap-2opt.html#quadratic-assignment-method-2opt

For the optional arguments partial_match and partial_guess, it is unmentioned that they should be sorted in the first column. Functionality in lines https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/main/scipy/optimize/_qap.py#L692 and https://github.com/scipy/scipy/blob/main/scipy/optimize/_qap.py#L686 assumes so.

Alternatively, do that sorting internally before processing partial_match and/or partial_guess.

### Idea or request for content:

Doing sorting internally before processing partial_match and/or partial_guess seems like the best option, but documentation change could be sufficient.

### Additional context (e.g. screenshots, GIFs)

Output of a quick testing script. One observes that without sorting the partial_match input, the contents aren't actually preserved.

![quad_assign_not_working_2](https://github.com/scipy/scipy/assets/137289884/b89527f4-6115-468f-a8ee-109a70fa994f)
